barbers, Brahmanas, potters, priests and astrologers.
[In the preceding chapter the author gives the rulership of the
nine triads of stars over the nine regions or quarters and the peoples
and countries situated therein. In this chapter he gives various
things and types of persons that are ruled by the 27 asterisms
severally. Rulership of the four Vanjas or classes is separately
given in 28-29 infra.]
Ttf^rart \
The following are ruled by RohinI : Observers of vows, mer-
chandises, kings, wealthy persons, Yogins, cartmen, cows, bulls,
aquatic animals, husbandmen, mountains and men in authority.
[Bhattotpala construes the word Panya as Panyavfttayah i.e.
merchants.]
win
The following are allotted to the constellation Mrgasiras :
Fragrant articles, garments, aquatic products, flowers, fruits, gems,
foresters (or forest-dwellers), birds, beasts, those who partake of
Soma juice (in sacrifices), musicians, lovers and carriers of letters.
